acceptance_targets: [chapter_03_f001] A reader comparing the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:75 with the Chapter 4 handoff finds one consistent manuscript total and no conflicting count anywhere in Chapter 3.

[chapter_03_f002] Reading the revised run from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:183, each major decoding beat changes the chapter in a visible way: either Gavin learns a genuinely new thing, misreads a concrete clue that worsens the obsession, or pays a newly rendered cost in school or friendship. If two adjacent beats only restate that the marks are organized but opaque, the passage fails.

findings.acceptance_test: A reader comparing the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:75 with the Chapter 4 handoff finds one consistent manuscript total and no conflicting count anywhere in Chapter 3.
findings.problem: The manuscript's sheet count is internally repeated as seventeen, but the chapter boundary handoff for Chapter 4 establishes fifty-seven sheets. This is a hard continuity contradiction on a central object and will make the next chapter read as if the manuscript changed size off-page.
findings.rewrite_direction: Align the manuscript count everywhere it appears in the discovery/removal beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:61, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:65, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:75 so Chapter 3 matches the canonical count used at the Chapter 4 boundary.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised run from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:183, each major decoding beat changes the chapter in a visible way: either Gavin learns a genuinely new thing, misreads a concrete clue that worsens the obsession, or pays a newly rendered cost in school or friendship. If two adjacent beats only restate that the marks are organized but opaque, the passage fails.
findings.problem: The decoding half of the chapter documents obsession, but too many consecutive beats cash out to the same result: the marks are structured, meaningful, and unreadable. Because the methods change more than the dramatic consequence changes, the chapter's second movement plateaus into a work log instead of escalating scene pressure to shortlist-level force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rebuild the failed-decoding sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:89, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:91, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:145, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:147, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_03.md:165 so each retained attempt produces a distinct turn: one new inference, one sharper false breakthrough, one clearer social or academic cost, and one relapse beat that lands harder than the prior loop. Cut or fuse any attempt that only restates unreadability.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central risk is tonal: it must sustain dark comedy, cosmic horror, and genuine emotional tenderness in the same narrative without any register canceling the others. Lean into the discomfort of juxtaposition. A scene where Gavin is mentally modeling ward-substrate decay while a classmate screams under the Cruciatus Curse is not a failure of empathy — it is the novel's core tension made visceral. The humor of the premise — that Voldemort's war is an irritating distraction from the real problem — must never be defused by making Gavin feel appropriately guilty at the right moments. He should feel guilty sometimes and not other times, and the reader should sit with the discomfort of a protagonist whose priorities have become genuinely alien. The cosmic material should be rendered with enough specificity that it is genuinely unsettling, not just philosophically interesting. Do not soften the ending: Gavin's refusal to intervene in the Battle of Hogwarts must be played straight, without a last-minute redemptive gesture or moral safety net.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in this novel is a particularly acute failure because the entire thematic engine runs on the difference between seeing something directly and not seeing it. Gavin's journey is toward unmediated perception. The prose must mirror that commitment. When the Carrows torture a student, render the torture. When Gavin is hit with the Cruciatus, render the pain in his body. When the cosmic threat presses against perception, render what it feels like — do not retreat to abstractions about 'something vast' or 'an incomprehensible presence.' Find the specific sensory detail that makes the incomprehensible land. Euphemism in a novel about the failure to see what is real is thematic self-sabotage.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, Babbling's academic register, or moments where a character is being pointedly formal. If a line reads stiffly, check contractions first.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter textures aggressively. A high-intensity chapter (Cruciatus detention, Duncan's torture) must be followed by a chapter with a different pressure profile — quieter, more internal, more comic, or more focused on intellectual work. No two consecutive chapters should share the same dominant emotional register, the same scene-structure (e.g., two chapters that each build to a confrontation), or the same pacing profile. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and the ratio of interiority to action. Some chapters should be mostly one extended scene; others should move across multiple locations and timeframes. The Christmas chapter and the work-routine chapters should breathe differently from the Carrow chapters and the battle chapter.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain, concrete, specific. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred over Latinate where both are available. Avoid poetic diction, literary allusion, and metaphors that call attention to their own cleverness. Metaphors should arise from Gavin's actual frame of reference: schoolwork, the castle, ordinary physical experience. When the First Art requires description, reach for precision over beauty — describe what it looks like, not what it means. The novel's most striking language should come from the collision between ordinary diction and extraordinary perception.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Gavin's perception throughout. The narrative voice has access to his thoughts, feelings, and sensory experience but not to anyone else's interiority. Other characters are known only through what Gavin observes. As the First Art restructures Gavin's cognition, the narrative's perceptual range should gradually expand — he begins noticing things no one else can see — but the voice remains grounded in his ordinary, slightly bewildered perspective. The narration should never become more articulate or literary than Gavin himself would be; the strangeness of what he perceives should press against the ordinariness of how he describes it.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied and responsive to scene pressure. Default rhythm is medium-paced prose with sentences of mixed length — some short, some compound, none habitually long. During scenes of intellectual discovery, let sentences extend as Gavin's attention extends, then cut short when comprehension fails. During Carrow scenes, prose should tighten and become more physical. During quiet scenes, allow room for observation and sensory detail without rushing toward the next event. Do not develop a signature rhythm that persists across all chapters — the rhythm should be the scene's rhythm, not the author's.
